steps.title: Spot the telling pattern
steps.content: The word Tellingly signals the crucial detail: all borrowed words in Mayan languages come from the Zoque branch, not the Mixe branch. This selective borrowing from just one branch of the family is the key signal.

steps.title: Match the pattern to the choices
steps.content: If the Mixe-Zoquean family had already split into two separate branches by Olmec times, the Olmec language (from one branch) would explain why only Zoque words were borrowed into Mayan languages. A contradicts this by placing Mixe dominance before Olmec times, which would eliminate Zoque words during that period, while C and D make unsupported claims beyond the passage. That matches choice B — B is correct.
